title: Control the Flow of CUI in Accordance with Approved Authorizations
description: control the flow of CUI in accordance with approved authorizations

checks.name: Information Flow Control Policies are Defined
checks.purpose: Verify that information flow control policies exist via Group Policy, Windows Firewall rules, or DLP configuration governing CUI movement

checks.name: Methods and Enforcement Mechanisms for Controlling CUI Flow are Defined
checks.purpose: Confirm that technical enforcement mechanisms such as Windows Defender Firewall, DLP policies, and removable media controls are active and configured

checks.name: Designated Sources and Destinations for CUI are Identified
checks.purpose: Verify that approved sources and destinations for CUI are reflected in firewall rules and domain join status restricting connectivity to known devices and subnets

checks.name: Authorizations for Controlling CUI Flow are Defined
checks.purpose: Confirm that CUI flow authorizations are reflected in Group Policy assignments, user rights, and DLP rule scoping to named groups or roles

checks.name: Information Flow Control Policies are Defined
checks.purpose: Verify that information flow control policies exist via Group Policy, Windows Firewall rules, or DLP configuration governing CUI movement on the server

checks.name: Methods and Enforcement Mechanisms for Controlling CUI Flow are Defined
checks.purpose: Confirm that technical enforcement mechanisms such as Windows Defender Firewall, IPSec policies, share permissions, and NTFS controls are active and configured

checks.name: Designated Sources and Destinations for CUI are Identified
checks.purpose: Verify that approved sources and destinations for CUI are reflected in firewall rules restricting inbound access to approved subnets and domain-joined devices only

checks.name: Authorizations for Controlling CUI Flow are Defined
checks.purpose: Confirm that CUI flow authorizations are reflected in AD group assignments, NTFS permissions, and share permissions scoped to named authorized groups

checks.name: Information Flow Control Policies are Defined
checks.purpose: Verify that information flow control policies exist via SELinux/AppArmor policy definitions and firewall rule configurations governing CUI movement

checks.name: Methods and Enforcement Mechanisms for Controlling CUI Flow are Defined
checks.purpose: Confirm that technical enforcement mechanisms such as iptables/nftables/firewalld, SELinux/AppArmor, and file permission controls are active and configured

checks.name: Designated Sources and Destinations for CUI are Identified
checks.purpose: Verify that approved sources and destinations for CUI are reflected in firewall rules restricting inbound and outbound connections to known subnets and systems

checks.name: Authorizations for Controlling CUI Flow are Defined
checks.purpose: Confirm that CUI flow authorizations are reflected in file ownership, group permissions, ACLs, and SELinux/AppArmor policy scoping to named users and roles

checks.name: Information Flow Control Policies are Defined
checks.purpose: Verify that information flow control policies exist via AppArmor policy definitions and firewall rule configurations governing CUI movement

checks.name: Methods and Enforcement Mechanisms for Controlling CUI Flow are Defined
checks.purpose: Confirm that technical enforcement mechanisms such as iptables/nftables/firewalld, AppArmor, and file permission controls are active and configured

checks.name: Designated Sources and Destinations for CUI are Identified
checks.purpose: Verify that approved sources and destinations for CUI are reflected in firewall rules restricting inbound and outbound connections to known subnets and systems

checks.name: Authorizations for Controlling CUI Flow are Defined
checks.purpose: Confirm that CUI flow authorizations are reflected in file ownership, group permissions, ACLs, and AppArmor policy scoping to named users and roles
